title: Siya Goyal's fate: A murder charge is one thing, proving it is another
content: Pune police allege a murder plot involving a fiancée and her boyfriend. Investigators claim they conspired to kill her fiancé by pushing him off a cliff. The case relies heavily on circumstantial evidence, which must be proven conclusively. Confessions made to police are generally inadmissible unless they lead to new evidence. The prosecution must establish an unbroken chain of evidence to secure a conviction.

title: 7-year-old raped and murdered in Ghaziabad; body found in basement of under-construction mall
content: A seven-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped and murdered in Ghaziabad. Her body was discovered in the basement of an under-construction mall. Family members claim she was lured with snacks before being assaulted. Police have detained several individuals for questioning regarding the incident. An investigation is underway, and an autopsy report is awaited.

title: Indian cricket set to undergo a coaching reset?
content: At least one prominent support staff member is likely to leave the Team India camp despite having an extension clause in the contract. The possible exits, unrelated to the recent T20I defeats, come amid dressing-room unease and growing differences between the coaching staff and selectors.

title: 'Prefers direct contact': MEA on New Zealand scribe's query on PM Modi
content: The Ministry of External Affairs explained Prime Minister Modi's direct engagement style with voters. He prefers connecting with the electorate without intermediaries, which is a political method. This approach has contributed to his electoral success and long tenure as Prime Minister. The visit aimed to strengthen the rule-based Indo-Pacific order with New Zealand.

title: Elon Musk speaks on thousands of SpaceX employees becoming millionaires after IPO
content: SpaceX's significant valuation has created thousands of employee millionaires.  The company recently became the world's fifth largest firm by market value.  This surge saw SpaceX briefly surpass Amazon and Microsoft in valuation.  Employee stock options and private liquidity events provided financial flexibility.  Elon Musk reiterated ambitious future goals for lunar and Martian colonization.

title: Kerala: Toddler dies after anaesthesia for lip cut; doctor booked for negligence
content: An anaesthetist faces negligence charges after a toddler died following anaesthesia administration.  The one-and-a-half-year-old boy received anaesthesia for a lip injury treatment.  His condition worsened, and he was moved to another hospital for further care.  The child unfortunately passed away while undergoing intensive treatment in the ICU.  Hospital authorities have denied the allegations and stated all possible treatment was provided.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Diljit's 'Satluj' removed from ZEE5's global catalogue: Reports
content: A government committee recommended continuing restrictions on Diljit Dosanjh's film Satluj.  The panel found the film's content concerning India's national security and sovereignty.  This decision follows the film's removal from streaming platforms in India and overseas.  Political leaders in Punjab have voiced differing opinions on the government's actions.  The film's future availability remains uncertain after these developments.

title: Why siblings raised together have different personalities: Science explains
content: Siblings develop distinct personalities despite sharing the same home environment. Genetics play a significant role in shaping individual temperaments and traits. Nonshared experiences and external influences further differentiate children's life paths. Sibling interactions and birth order also contribute to personality development. Ultimately, a complex web of factors creates unique individuals from the same family.

title: Michael Rubin's White Party puts $45m Hamptons mansion in spotlight
content: Wealthy guests from Michael Rubin's White Party are exploring a $45 million mansion. This luxurious oceanfront property is located near Rubin's own Hamptons estate. At least four attendees have scheduled private viewings of the impressive residence. The broker noted significant interest generated by the exclusive summer event. This renewed attention highlights the Hamptons' enduring appeal for affluent buyers.

title: CEO of one of America's biggest defense tech startup has message for OpenAI, Anthropic
content: Anduril CEO Brian Schimpf believes now is not the right time for an initial public offering.  He noted that tech valuations are currently very high and potentially irrational.  Modern conflicts increasingly target economic infrastructure and not just military assets.  Schimpf also warned that many companies are dangerously overvalued.  This trend could lead to significant negative consequences for these businesses.

title: Army to induct 450 new Carl Gustaf M4 rocket launchers — lighter, deadlier tank-killers
content: The Army will induct 450 new Carl Gustaf M4 rocket launchers. This lighter system improves infantry firepower, especially in mountainous regions. The weapon operates effectively in extreme temperatures from deserts to frontiers. A variety of domestic ammunition provides versatile combat capabilities. India's first foreign-owned defense unit now produces these launchers.

title: Who are Lamine Yamal's parents? Meet Mounir Nasraoui and Sheila Ebana
content: Lamine Yamal's parents, Mounir Nasraoui from Morocco and Sheila Ebana from Equatorial Guinea, both immigrated to Spain and raised Yamal in the greater Barcelona area under modest financial conditions. After separating when Yamal was around three years old, his mother raised him in Rocafonda, Mataró, while working to make ends meet. His name honors two men who helped his family during a period of hardship. Despite his Spanish nationality and international football career with La Roja, Yamal proudly represents his Moroccan and Equatorial Guinean heritage, wearing his parents' national flags on his boots at every match.

title: Stefon Diggs case: Who is accuser Christopher Blake Griffith?
content: Stefon Diggs, the former New England Patriots’ wide receiver, has been dealing with many challenging things in his life for the past few months, ever since he was released by the team after losing in the Super Bowl, a few months ago. While his high profile break up with Cardi B had sparked chaos, the controversial assault allegation made by a man named Christopher Blake Griffith has shocked millions.

title: Blackhawks, Jets eye $38.5M Canucks winger in latest trade buzz
content: The Vancouver Canucks’ winger Jake DeBrusk has been a regular name concerning trade speculations, ahead of the new season. As the team seeks a roster rebuild, a number of teams have been linked to him. Amid all the mess, the Chicago Blackhawks and the Winnipeg Jets have emerged as potential landing spots.

title: 'People don't want us to win': Argentina head coach fires back at FIFA favouritism claims
content: Argentina's coach Lionel Scaloni rejects FIFA favouritism allegations.  He states these claims provide extra motivation for his players.  Scaloni mentioned such accusations have followed Argentina for decades.  He believes VAR technology makes manipulation extremely difficult.  The team prepares for their quarter-final match against Switzerland.

title: Six dead in under an hour: How Telangana man wiped out two families in one night
content: A man accused in a POCSO case allegedly killed six people on Friday night. The victims included the teenage girl, her mother, and her grandmother. He then murdered his own wife and their two young sons. The accused informed his parents after committing the crimes. A search operation is underway for the absconding accused.

title: Chicken instead of mutton sparks wedding brawl in Bihar, several baraatis injured
content: A wedding celebration in Saharsa district turned violent after a food dispute.  The groom's family expected mutton but received chicken, sparking an argument.  This disagreement escalated into a physical confrontation between the two families.  Several individuals from the groom's side sustained injuries during the clash.  Police are investigating the incident after receiving information about the altercation.

title: 'Public's rightful grain is being looted': Kharge attacks BJP over alleged MP ethanol scam
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge criticized the Madhya Pradesh government for alleged corruption. He highlighted a Rs 1,200 crore rice scam involving diverted grain. This rice was meant for malnourished children and women. An investigation is underway after a complaint about undelivered rice. Kharge also mentioned land scam allegations against the chief minister.

title: NSE Indices launches Nifty500 Ahimsa Index: How the 'ethical' benchmark works
content: NSE Indices has launched the Nifty500 Ahimsa Index, tracking companies aligned with non-violence principles. This new index targets investors seeking animal welfare considerations in their portfolios. Companies are screened using the Ahimsagain Foundation's AIM framework for eligibility. The index offers a transparent benchmark for responsible investment products. It will serve as a base for ETFs and index funds.

title: After collecting crores from investors, Hyderabad couple go 'missing' in Switzerland
content: A missing couple from Hyderabad is now under investigation for financial dealings.  They allegedly collected several crores from multiple investors before their departure.  The couple left their home for Switzerland on June 22, according to reports.  Their daughter filed a missing person's report after they became untraceable.  Police are exploring various angles in this developing financial case.

title: No Kohli or Tendulkar : Harmanpreet Kaur becomes first Indian to...
content: Harmanpreet Kaur achieved a unique milestone by scoring a fifty at Lord's. She became the first Indian to score fifties in Tests, ODIs, and T20Is there. Smriti Mandhana also contributed significantly with a score of 83 runs. India posted 285 runs in their first innings after a strong partnership. England finished the opening day on 21 for 1, trailing by 264 runs.

title: Missing trail: Why Ram temple donation theft may never be fully quantified
content: Four individuals with prior association to the Ram temple were arrested for donation theft. These accused had been involved with temple work before cash counting began. A private agency managed cash, and these volunteers were deputed as counting agents. Significant amounts of cash and jewelry were recovered from the arrested individuals. Police investigations are focusing on this group of four accused.

title: Home loan or top-up loan: How to choose right option for construction & renovation
content: Homeowners often need extra funds for property improvements and unexpected expenses. A home loan finances new construction or major renovations on a property. Top-up loans provide additional money for existing homeowners with a reliable repayment history. These loans are secured against residential property and offer flexible repayment options. Understanding the differences helps homeowners choose the right financing for their needs.

title: Gunfire, panic, trembling voices: Viral videos capture Gurgaon encounter from balconies
content: An intense late-night police encounter unfolded in Gurgaon's upscale Sushant Lok colony. Five alleged hired hitmen exchanged gunfire with police after arriving at a businessman's home. Investigators believe the group was sent by a UK-based gangster over an extortion demand. Residents heard relentless gunfire and saw police vehicles seal off the residential lane. The armed confrontation lasted approximately two minutes, causing residents to stay indoors.

title: Truce and fire: The ghost of Versailles haunts Trump. But is Iran overplaying its hand?
content: President Trump declared the June 17 ceasefire with Iran over, ordering fresh US strikes. Iran responded by targeting tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions. The conflict's tempo is now largely controlled by Iran's ability to disrupt oil shipments. Both sides face pressures, with neither in a decisive position to end the war. Managed instability with periodic attacks and negotiations is the likely immediate outcome.

title: 'Some rallies never end': Tendulkar's heartfelt message to Federer goes viral
content: Sachin Tendulkar and Roger Federer shared a heartwarming reunion at Wimbledon this year.  Shubman Gill also experienced the prestigious event from the Royal Box for the first time.  Tendulkar also met with Brian Lara, recalling their famous cricket rivalries.  Several other prominent athletes and sports figures attended the championships.  Alexander Zverev secured his place in the Wimbledon final after a strong performance.

title: 60 bullets in 2 mins: Plush Gurgaon society turns battlefield; class 11 student among 4 dead
content: Four hired hitmen died and one was critically injured during a police encounter. The shooters were sent by gangster Deepak Nandal for extortion. Three police officers sustained bullet injuries in the fierce gunfight. The encounter occurred after the hitmen fired at a businessman's house. This incident marks a significant escalation in extortion crimes in Delhi-NCR.

title: 'If this happiness improves my father's health ... ': Prabhsimran on India call-up
content: Prabhsimran Singh has finally earned his maiden India call-up after back-to-back impressive IPL seasons with Punjab Kings. In this exclusive interview, the opener recalls his father's emotional reaction, explains how consistency and converting starts into big scores changed his career, and reflects on the role of Shreyas Iyer, Ricky Ponting and Yuvraj Singh in shaping his journey to the Indian team.

title: Bengal govt forms 9-member panel to examine draft UCC bill; legislation may be tabled in August
content: West Bengal has formed a nine-member committee to review the Uniform Civil Code draft. Retired Supreme Court judge Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai will head this expert panel. The proposed legislation aims to create a uniform legal framework for personal civil matters. The draft bill is expected to be introduced during the Assembly's August session.

title: What drove BJP to pull its Bankipur nominee days before Prashant Kishor face-off
content: BJP's Abhishek Kumar Sinha withdrew from the Bankipur bypoll citing family reasons. The party then fielded Neeraj Kumar Sinha as its new candidate for the contest. Opposition parties claimed the BJP was nervous about a potential defeat in the upcoming election. Jan Suraaj founder Prashant Kishor is campaigning against the ruling party. Polling for the Bankipur bypoll is scheduled for July 30.

title: How China can bring Taiwan to its knees without firing a shot
content: China may employ a gray zone strategy to isolate Taiwan economically and psychologically. This approach involves naval, cyber, and information operations below war thresholds. A blockade would restrict vital imports, impacting Taiwan's energy and supply chains. Cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns would further destabilize the island's society. Taiwanese resilience drills prepare for a multifaceted siege beyond conventional military conflict.

title: INS Mahendragiri commissioned: A milestone on India’s road to a 200-ship, atmanirbhar Navy
content: The Indian Navy aims for a 200-ship fleet by 2035 through self-reliance. Indigenous design and construction are key to this ambitious naval expansion. The navy is enhancing its capabilities in float, move, and fight systems. This strategic push secures maritime trade and projects regional stability. Investment in new technologies supports a multi-domain operational force.

title: Mikel Merino creates FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player to...
content: Mikel Merino scored another decisive late goal for Spain against Belgium. This victory propelled La Roja into the FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinals. Merino's impactful substitute appearances have now made World Cup history. Spain will face France in their next crucial tournament match. The team's unbeaten run in regular time now extends significantly.

title: Murder rampage: Man out on bail kills Pocso victim, her family & then his own; manhunt on
content: A farmer allegedly murdered six people in Shabad and his village. He killed a teenage girl, her mother, and grandmother after a POCSO complaint. The accused then murdered his wife and two young sons at their home. He drove to a lake and killed the girl before returning home. Police have launched a manhunt for the absconding accused.

title: 'Stone pelting, highway blockade': Protest erupts after BJP denies leader bypoll ticket
content: Supporters of Narottam Mishra protested the BJP's decision to deny him a ticket. They blocked the Jhansi-Gwalior highway, demanding the party reconsider its candidate choice. This action caused significant traffic disruptions for several hours on Friday evening. The BJP had announced Ashutosh Tiwari as their candidate for the Datia by-election. Local party office bearers also submitted resignations in solidarity with Mishra.

title: Akshay Kumar details sleep, workout and diet; offers 8-8-8 rule guide
content: Akshay Kumar exemplifies peak fitness, adhering to strict routines and a nutritious diet. He emphasizes the importance of seven to eight hours of sleep along with an early start every day. His fitness activities span martial arts, yoga, and bodyweight training for enhanced agility. Adopting a vegan diet and practicing weekly fasting significantly boost his overall wellness.

title: Hyderabad couple disappear after telling daughters they were flying to Switzerland; police probe alleged ₹50-crore chit fund scam
content: Police probe whether the businessman and his wife ever left India while examining allegations of a ₹50-crore chit fund scheme; no fraud complaint has been registered so far.

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Cloudy skies to persist in Delhi; highways blocked after landslides in hill States
content: Widespread rainfall lashed large parts of Uttar Pradesh on July 10, bringing daytime temperatures below normal at several places, with heavy to very heavy rainfall being forecast at isolated places

title: PM Modi in New Zealand LIVE: 'India-NZ Waka' ready to embark on new voyage together, says Modi at diaspora event
content: India and New Zealand elevate ties to "strategic partnership" during PM Modi's visit to Auckland in the third and final leg of his three-nation tour

title: Born in Sri Lanka, brought up in India, residing in the US: IT employee faces threat of being declared ‘stateless’
content: Madras High Court directs Indian Embassy in Washington DC to keep in abeyance till July 22 a notice instructing him to surrender his passport

title: Young readers reflect a country’s future, says Home Minister Amit Shah
content: “The future of a country can be assessed by seeing how crowded its libraries are and whether young people are there says,” Home Minister Amit Shah

title: Boat carrying several Indian tourists capsizes in Vietnam; rescue efforts under way
content: Exact details of incident near Phu Quoc island yet to be ascertained; Indian Embassy in Hanoi has set up control rooms

title: Two youngsters died after falling into a quarry
content: The incident occurred around 10:26 a.m. at Shaikh Khadan in Ketki Pada, an area under the jurisdiction of the National Park in Mumbai’s Borivali area

title: Security tightened at Red Fort after bomb threat call; no suspicious objects found
content: A bomb threat call was received at the Mumbai police control room, which was forwarded to the Delhi Police control room

title: Bengaluru Traffic Police’s 101-skywalk proposal moves towards implementation
content: The list of proposed skywalks features several notorious junctions, such as T.C. Palya Junction, Budigere Junction, Santhe Circle in Yelahanka, Aurobindo Junction in Jayanagar, Iblur Junction, and Jalahalli Cross

title: Central Tribal university focusing on campus recruitment, entrepreneurship and research says in-charge VC
content: The youngsters who attended for the all-India level Common University Entrance Test (CUET) are eligible to get admissions in the University

title: Bihar led 'second Independence movement' during Emergency: C.P. Radhakrishnan
content: “Bihar had not only played a crucial role in the country’s freedom struggle but also led from the front to spearhead the second independence movement during the Emergency,” says C.P. Radhakrishnan

title: Debris, demolished stalls greet visitors at Hyderabad’s DLF food street after Cyberabad’s encroachment removal drive
content: DLF food street witnessed the demolition of 48 temporary structures, canopies, signboards and stalls that had extended onto footpaths and public spaces

title: Safe Footpath Campaign runs into conflict with street vendors in Bengaluru
content: Hundreds of street vendors have been evicted and the wares they were hawking seized, as part of the drive in the past one week.

title: Over 300 employees hit as ecosystem company Talrop shuts offices across Kerala
content: Affected employees staged a protest march to the company’s head office at Thrikkakara on July 11, demanding clearance of dues

title: Assam government to dismiss employees practising polygamy
content: In his Budget speech, Assam Finance Minister Jayanta Malla Baruah said the government would amend the 1964 service rules to make employees practising polygamy liable for dismissal

title: UN Special Rapporteurs raise concerns over SIR and use of AI-driven models for exclusion of minorities
content: UN Report flags multiple issues with SIR including alleged use of opaque AI-driven systems which lack remedies and has a political narrative aimed at exclusion of minorities

title: Security forces launch search operations in Manipur after two Assam Rifles personnel killed
content: On July 10, Assam Rifles said the paramilitary force, in coordination with the Border Security Force and Manipur Police, commenced a large-scale search operation in the Nungshang area

title: Slashed perks among factors affecting mental health of Assam’s tea garden executives
content: A new study has identified restricted decision latitude, referring to a severe lack of control, autonomy, and flexibility, as the most frequently occurring category of workplace stress risk factors

title: AIADMK criticises Vijay for visiting Karur with an eye on byelections
content: D. Jayakumar said the Chief Minister could have paid solatium on his own, instead of providing government jobs to the families of those of who lost their lives in Karur stampede

title: Bengal CM hands over job letter, ₹25 lakh cheque to kin of man lynched after girl’s rape, murder
content: The Chief Minister handed over an appointment letter for the post of civic volunteer to Indrajit Mondal's elder brother and a ₹25 lakh cheque to his parents

title: Tussle for Punjab Congress chief post: Bhupesh Baghel meets leaders from Channi camp
content: Former Minister Gurpreet Kangar said the meeting was being held specifically to discuss whether Amrinder Singh Raja Warring’s leadership was acceptable to them or not; Bhupesh Baghel had already ruled out any change in the State party leadership

title: Datia byelection: Narottam Mishra's supporters clash with M.P. Police, block highway after BJP ticket snub
content: The nearly 12-hour blockade on NH-44 by over 3,000 protesters left the Datia Superintendent of Police and several police personnel injured before security forces cleared the highway and arrested some agitators, officials said.

title: Organ trade racket: ED expands probe, summons doctors part of Kerala hospitals’ internal evaluation committees
content: Financial records are also under scrutiny to determine if doctors received illicit payments in the form of kickbacks or commissions to fast-track approvals

title: J&K being punished on Statehood status because people voted NC to power: Omar Abdullah
content: Omar Abdullah invited all the J&K parties to join the protest at Jantar Mantar on the first day of the monsoon session of the Parliament

title: Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan visits Kollur, offers special pooja at Mookambika Temple
content: A staunch devotee of Kollur Sri Mookambika, Mr. Satheesan visited Kollur for the first time after assuming office as Kerala CM.

title: Repair work at water treatment plant disrupts drinking water supply in Shivamogga
content: Officers of Shivamogga City Corporation and the Karnataka Urban Water Supply and Drainage Board issued a statement that there was a leakage in the clariflocculator that plays a major role in water treatment.

title: I am not Gandhi; be your own hero: Wangchuk amid indefinite fast
content: "Many people call me the Gandhi of the 21st century or a modern Gandhi. Others call me a hero. These comments make me uncomfortable. I am neither Gandhi nor a hero. I am just an ordinary citizen who has tried to fulfil his responsibilities,"says Sonam Wangchuk

title: The unfulfilled solar promise of India and Germany
content: Three years after both countries signed a landmark agreement to facilitate the migration of Indian solar technicians to Germany, not a single such technician has made the journey; the experience exposes a wider disconnect between ambitious labour mobility agreements and the realities of skill development, labour market demand and the aspirations of India’s emerging green workforce

title: World Records Union recognises Biju BP from Kerala for his all-India motorcycle ride with a mission
content: A former banker, Thiruvananthapuram-native Biju PB, won the recognition  for riding across the States and Union Territories, championing the cause of empowerment of girls through education

title: Wayanad debris slip: search intensified for lone missing project manager
content: ​Mr. Rana, a project manager from Himachal Pradesh, remains unaccounted for after a massive debris slide struck the under-construction Wayanad twin-tunnel road project at Kalladi

title: 14 invasive plants behind grassland loss in one of India’s first tiger reserves
content: Data provided by the Forest Department in Assam’s Bodoland Territorial Council reveal that the relative loss in grassland cover in Manas Tiger Reserve in 30 years since 1990 was 43.59%

title: TTDC launches ₹99 chicken biriyani amid rising restaurant prices
content: The ₹99 offer is for chicken biriyani. Each serving comprises approximately 450–500 grams of chicken biriyani and is accompanied by onion raita and brinjal gravy

title: Nine in ten: Why Jammu and Kashmir’s private hospitals have India’s highest C-Section rate
content: In India, medical experts attribute the increase in C-sections to a combination of more institutional deliveries, improved detection of high-risk pregnancies, increasing maternal age and medical complications, greater use of assisted reproductive technologies, previous C-sections, and the rapid growth of private-sector obstetric care

title: PM Modi tours New Zealand sports innovation showcase
content: India and New Zealand elevated their ties to a strategic partnership and set a five-year target to double their annual bilateral trade in goods and services to ₹35,000 crore by 2030

title: Police squad attacked in Kozhikode; five suspected goons arrested
content: According to police sources, the confrontation took place at Peruvayal near Mavoor in Kozhikode city around 9 a.m. when the police squad, acting on precise location data, zeroed in on the suspects at a local villa

title: How two ‘objectionable’ books triggered a crackdown on J&K’s varsity and college libraries | Explained
content: Around 251 copies of these books were already circulated among the schools in J&K. The J&K administration said the introduction of these books was a result of  “serious negligence, dereliction of duty and lack of proper due diligence”, and blamed the members of the Sub Committee, which approved it.

title: Wayanad debris slip: Kerala constitutes expert panel to study cause of accident; report to be submitted in a month
content: The committee comprises Dr. C.P. Rajendran, geologist; Dr. Vishnudas, ecologist; Dr. P. Pugazhendi, Chief Wildlife Warden, Kerala; the Secretary, Public Works Department; and the Secretary, Revenue and Disaster Management Department (convenor)

title: India joins the FIFA momentum
content: The FIFA World Cup excitement continues to build in India as fans gear up for four high-stakes knockout clashes.

title: INS Mahendragiri: All you need to know about the Indian Navy’s new indigenous stealth frigate
content: The frigate takes its name from the Mahendragiri mountain range in the Eastern Ghats, symbolising strength, resilience and determination

title: How a single ₹500 counterfeit currency helped police unravel a larger syndicate
content: The kingpin and the accused arrested in Bengaluru allegedly printed the counterfeit currency and roped in foot soldiers to circulate the notes

title: K.C. Venugopal seeks SC-monitored probe into Ayodhya Ram temple donations scam
content: Congress leader alleges SIT inquiry is a “sham”, accuses BJP governments of shielding those behind the alleged misappropriation

title: Pawan Kalyan undergoes shoulder surgery in Mumbai
content: Surgery for the left shoulder, which also has its rotator cuff injured, will be performed after two months, according to a press release

title: Standard KAVACH commissioned on Solapur–Wadi section of Central Railway
content: 149.476-km Solapur–Wadi route becomes first in the Solapur Division of Central Railway to get indigenous train protection system

title: PM Modi must take responsibility for alleged misappropriation Ram Temple Trust: Rajeev Gowda
content: “All the activities related to the temple trust, all the appointments were done through the Union Government, and Home Minister Amit Shah was central to the appointment,” says Professor Gowda

title: Decision pending on direct purchase of land in Ayyankuzhi for Kerala waste management project
content: “While acquisition through the direct purchase route will be swift, it would be more protracted under the Land Acquisition Act,” district administration sources said

title: On a bird quest: Doctors in Chennai turn avid bird photographers
content: It was in 2017 that Dr. Deepthi Cheran learned the ropes of photography from Rathika Ramasamy, a wildlife photographer. Since then, she has travelled to Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Uttarakhand, Rajasthan and Tamil Nadu in search of birds and has captured images of 300 to 400 species of birds so far.

title: INS Mahendragiri commissioned; Defence Minister says future wars will still be won by resolve and soldiers, not just AI
content: Rajnath Singh also said Andhra Pradesh has emerged as a new powerhouse of India’s defence and aerospace manufacturing

title: Jharkhand Assembly election: ‘Dented in Haryana, denied in Maharashtra, defeated in Jharkhand’
content: Opposition leaders see victory as a verdict on BJP’s arrogance and against controversial laws such as the Citizenship (Amendment) Act

title: U.P. govt. changes State university name ‘partially’
content: The varsity would now be called Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti Bhasha Vishwavidyalaya (language university), the government said in a statement

title: Economic Survey 2019-20: ‘No evidence of wrong estimation of growth’
content: The Economic Survey also added that since 10% increase in new firm creation increases district-level GDP growth by 1.8%.

title: CM Yogi claims Samajwadi Party, Congress allowed namaz at Ayodhya’s Hanumangarhi
content: Those pretending to have faith committed this sin, whereas the BJP established Ayodhya as the cultural capital of Sanatan Dharma, says Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath

title: Vijay announces memorial for Karur stampede victims, faults police for not informing him of the crowd
content: Speaking at a public meeting held on the outskirts of Karur, he says the tragedy could have been averted had the police alerted him to the gathering during his election campaign at Velusamypuram on September 27, 2025

title: Four of family found dead at house in Bengaluru’s Kottigepalya
content: Police said their preliminary investigation suggests that Prashanth is suspected to have fatally attacked the three family members before his death.

title: Tamil Nadu’s biryani binge keeps cash registers ringing
content: Recording revenues of ₹4,500 crore per year, there’s more space at the table, say established players

title: Ayodhya verdict | Temple at disputed site, alternative land for mosque, rules Supreme Court
content: Centre told to formulate a scheme within three months and set up a trust to manage the property and construct a temple.

title: Narendra Modi breaks silence on Kathua, Unnao rape cases: ‘Our daughters will get justice’
content: “I want to assure the nation that no criminal will be spared. Justice will be done. Our daughters will get justice,” he said at an event to inaugurate the B.R. Ambedkar memorial in New Delhi.

title: Bengal Forms High-Level Committee To Review Draft Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: A government notification issued on Friday said the committee was formed in view of the "wide ramifications and voluminous nature" of the proposed legislation.

title: Mountains Of Cash, Weapons Found At Don Baba Farzan's Home In Maharashtra
content: In the 1990s, Don Baba Farzan established a strong influence and terror in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar and its surrounding areas.

title: 'Goes Against India's Sovereignty': Government Panel Backs Ban On 'Satluj'
content: The Honey Trehan-directed film depicts the life of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, who investigated the cremation of thousands of unidentified bodies in Punjab between 1984 and 1994

title: Falling Debris Puts 400 Families In Greater Noida's Janata Flats At Risk
content: Plaster has been peeling off walls, while chhajjas (balconies) have been frequently collapsing, turning the housing complex into a potential hazard.

title: China's New Mega Dam Near Indian Border Poses Huge Geological Risk: Report
content: China is building the world's largest hydroelectric dam in the lower parts of the Yarlung Tsangpo River in Tibet, about 50 kilometres from the border with India.

title: Why Vladimir Putin's 'Shadow Fleet' Tankers Are Taking Longer Routes
content: Russia's sanctioned oil tankers are avoiding the English Channel after Britain seized a vessel from Moscow's "shadow fleet" last month.

title: How India's Indo-Pacific Strategy Is Countering China's 'String of Pearls'
content: While China appears intent on extending its footprint through ports, infrastructure projects and strategic investments under its "String of Pearls" policy, India is quietly pursuing another vision for the region.

title: Delivery Boy, 21, Kills Girlfriend, Dumps Body In A Hilly Area In Mumbai
content: The victim's identity was established by questioning local residents, verifying missing person records, analysing CCTV footage and examining technical evidence, the official said.

title: 75% Indigenous INS Mahendragiri Commissioned Into Indian Navy
content: INS Mahendragiri can be fitted with the BrahMos surface-to-surface missile, among the world's fastest and most lethal cruise missiles.

title: Triple Murder In Bengaluru: Man Kills His Family Of 3, Then Hangs Self
content: The victims have been identified as Mangalamma (55), Prashant's mother and a garment factory worker; Nanjamma, his grandmother who worked as a sweeper; and Satish (50), his unmarried brother-in-law, who worked as a plumber.

title: 'No Longer Part Of Probe': Suspect Arrested In Ex-UK MP Murder Case Released
content: Ann Widdecombe, 78, was known for her socially conservative views, first as a junior minister in Conservative prime minister John Major's 1992-1997 government.

title: 'Lots Of Opportunities' For India, Australia In AI, Tech: Minister To NDTV
content: At the heart of PM Narendra Modi's visit to Australia is the newly unveiled PACTS framework, an agreement built around supply chain resilience, critical technology sharing, cybersecurity and defence research collaboration.

title: Mumbai Civic Body Office's Roof Collapses. Building Was Inaugurated 6 Months Ago
content: The roof collapsed outside the cabin of the assistant commissioner at the BMC's K/North Ward office in Jogeshwari.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Explosions Heard In Tehran For 2nd Straight Day
content: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: 'Secret' Photos Of Team Uddhav Rebel MPs' Meet In Delhi Before Defection Surface
content: On June 17, Team Uddhav rebel MPs met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla at his residence in Delhi secretly and submitted a letter seeking separate recognition.

title: Court Raps Cops Over Jailed Gangster Anmol Bishnoi's Custody In Baba Siddique's Murder
content: Siddique (66), a former Maharashtra minister, was shot dead by three assailants outside his son Zeeshan's office in Mumbai's Bandra East area on the night of October 12, 2024.

title: Two Kolkata Top Cops Suspended Over 2025 Remarks Against Suvendu Adhikari
content: The two officers suspended are Deputy Commissioner of Police III (North Division) Bidhan Saha and Deputy Commissioner of Police (Port Division II) Partha Pratim Das

title: Court Cancels Shraddha Walkar Murder Case Hearing For Accused's Masters Exam
content: The prosecution alleged that Aaftab Amin Poonawala strangled Shraddha Walkar, chopped her body into multiple pieces, stored them in a refrigerator and disposed of them over several days in different parts of Delhi.

title: Cancer patient dead, her plea for drug listed 57 times for hearing
content: a petition for access to a life-saving breast cancer medicine has been listed for final hearing 57 times . the petitioner died in the early stage of the case, after which the HC decided to continue it suo motu . it is one of the most common cancers among women in India .
